key_drivers: Iran War / Hormuz Strait Disruption: Strait of Hormuz remains closed, causing largest-ever oil supply disruption (~5.1 mb/d draw Q2); Trump extended ceasefire indefinitely but no peace deal reached
Hawkish Fed Hold: FOMC April 28-29 meeting expected to hold rates at 3.50-3.75% (99.5% probability); J.P. Morgan sees no cuts in 2026, possible hike
Slowing US Growth: Atlanta Fed GDPNow Q1 2026 at 1.2% (down from 1.9% in April); growth headwind for risk assets and small caps
SOX Semiconductor Surge: SOX up 27% in April (best month ever) on Hormuz ceasefire hopes; record $5.45B inflows into SOXX/SMH ETFs
Flat DXY / Balanced VIX: Dollar flat at ~98.30 DXY; VIX at 19.16 (balanced zone 15-20); MOVE index falling (-4.81% weekly) = calming rate volatility

fallbacks_used: World Gold Council for ETF flows
overnight_changes: Trump extended US-Iran ceasefire indefinitely (April 22 AM)
EIA crude inventory draw larger than expected (April 22 release)
SOX futures up pre-market on ceasefire extension

ECB Rate Decision April 30, 2026 (expected: hold at 2.00%)
